claim: Fresh oracle-regime calibration reaches near-target aggregate marginal coverage across all seven benchmarks.
evidence: Oracle aggregate coverage lies within +/-0.02 of 0.90.

claim: Global coverage can conceal serious local seed-regime undercoverage.
Stale evidence can fail by undercoverage or excessive conservatism.
evidence: MNIST-family/CIFAR10 undercoverage; SplitCIFAR100/CORe50 broad conservative sets.

claim: Wrong regime recall can invalidate an otherwise valid calibrator.
scope: Oracle quantiles selected by exported context.

evidence: CORe50 fresh coverage 0.9011, mean set 28.67/50, singleton ACTION 0.15%.

claim: Fresh calibration repairs a weak backbone.
evidence: Coverage can be restored using broad sets without improving top-1 competence.
scope: Calibration is not a learning optimizer.

claim: The singleton gate is a universal deployment policy or a formal lower bound on all forms of actionability.
evidence: It is a conservative common baseline; deployment policies are cost-dependent.

claim: v0.3.2 implements online label-free regime detection and adaptive recalibration.
evidence: CUSUM/BOCPD and streaming buffers remain roadmap items.

claim: The 90% target is a conditional guarantee for every task, seed, class, or time interval.
evidence: Split conformal gives marginal coverage under exchangeability.
